title: moraceae
intro: The Moraceae rank, also known as the mulberry family, is a botanical family of flowering plants. It is a group of approximately 48-50 genera and over 1,000 species of plants that are primarily native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. The Moraceae family includes a diverse range of plants, such as figs, mulberries, and banyans, which are often characterized by their large, lobed leaves and small, inconspicuous flowers. These plants often thrive in warm, humid environments and can be found growing in a variety of habitats, from forests to backyards.
